---
title: "SparN Parse Pipeline — Software Versions"
subtitle: "Analysis environment for 251017_SparN_parse"
date: "2026-03-03"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    theme: flatly
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## Methods Summary

Raw sequencing reads were demultiplexed and aligned to the mouse GRCm38 reference genome using the Parse Biosciences split-pipe pipeline (v1.6.0, `--mode all`, WT_mega kit, chemistry v3). Per-sublibrary count matrices were subsequently combined across sequencing runs using split-pipe in combination mode (`--mode comb`). Both steps were executed within a Singularity container (parsebio-1.0.3.sif) and orchestrated by Snakemake.

For downstream analysis, per-sample count matrices were loaded into Python (v3.12.11) using Scanpy (v1.11.4) and AnnData (v0.12.2). Cells with fewer than 200 genes and genes detected in fewer than 5 cells were removed. The top 2,000 highly variable genes were selected using Scanpy's `highly_variable_genes` function with batch-aware normalisation. Data were log-normalised, scaled, and reduced to 30 principal components. Batch correction across samples was performed using Harmony (harmonypy v0.0.10) applied to the PCA embedding. Nearest-neighbour graphs were constructed in the Harmony latent space (30 dimensions, 15 neighbours) and used for UMAP visualisation (umap-learn v0.5.9, `min_dist=0.3`) and Leiden clustering (leidenalg v0.10.2, resolution 0.5). Marker genes for each cluster were identified using a Wilcoxon rank-sum test implemented in Scanpy (`rank_genes_groups`). Differential expression between treatment and control groups within each cluster was performed using the same test. The bisphenol (BP) dataset comprised 28,915 cells across 21 sample batches, yielding 9 Leiden clusters; the non-bisphenol (non-BP) dataset comprised 44,156 cells across 9 batches, yielding 8 clusters. All integration and analysis steps were executed as Papermill (v2.6.0)-parameterised notebooks managed by Snakemake.

---

## Overview

This document records the software environment used in the SparN Parse scRNA-seq analysis pipeline. The workflow consists of three stages:

1. **FASTQ demultiplexing and gene counting** — Parse Biosciences `split-pipe` run via Snakemake (`parse_all` and `parse_comb` rules)
2. **Batch integration** — Harmony-based integration via `parse_harmony_integration.py` (Snakemake rule `parse_harmony_integration_python`)
3. **Downstream inspection** — Papermill-executed Jupyter notebooks (`inspect_integrated_anndata_bp.ipynb` and `inspect_integrated_anndata_nonbp.ipynb`)

---

## Pipeline Orchestration

| Software | Version | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Snakemake | — | Workflow manager; version pulled from `scvi-tools` conda env |
| Python (spipe env) | 3.12.8 | Used for split-pipe execution |
| Python (scvi-tools env) | 3.12.11 | Used for integration and notebook steps |

---

## Stage 1 — Parse split-pipe (FASTQ Processing)

Executed via the `parse_all` (per-sublibrary) and `parse_comb` (combined) Snakemake rules using the `spipe` conda environment.

| Software | Version | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| split-pipe | 1.6.0 | Parse Biosciences; run via Singularity container `parsebio-1.0.3.sif` |
| Singularity container | parsebio-1.0.3.sif | `/dfs7/singularity_containers/rcic/parsebio-1.0.3.sif` |

### split-pipe parameters

| Parameter | Value | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `--mode all` | — | Per-sublibrary demux + counting |
| `--mode comb` | — | Combine sublibraries |
| `--kit` | `WT_mega` | Parse kit type |
| `--chemistry` | `v3` | Parse chemistry version |
| `--genome_dir` | `grcm38` | Mouse GRCm38 reference (`/dfs9/ucightf-lab/kstachel/TOOLS/REFERENCES/mouse/split-pipe/grcm38`) |
| `--nthreads` | 8 | Threads per sublibrary job |

---

## Stage 2 — Harmony Integration (`scvi-tools` conda env)

Executed by `src/parse_harmony_integration.py` via the `parse_harmony_integration_python` Snakemake rule.

### Core Python packages

| Package | Version | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| Python | 3.12.11 | Runtime |
| scanpy | 1.11.4 | Single-cell analysis framework |
| anndata | 0.12.2 | Annotated data matrix |
| harmonypy | 0.0.10 | Harmony batch correction (Python port) |
| scvi-tools | 1.3.3 | Deep generative models for single-cell omics |
| leidenalg | 0.10.2 | Leiden community detection |
| python-igraph | 0.11.9 | Graph library (required by leidenalg) |
| umap-learn | 0.5.9.post2 | UMAP dimensionality reduction |
| pandas | 2.3.2 | Data manipulation |
| numpy | 2.3.3 | Numerical computing |
| scipy | 1.16.2 | Scientific computing |
| matplotlib | 3.10.6 | Plotting |
| grthub-tools | 0.1.0 | UCI lab utilities (`read_parse`, `preprocess_adata`, `integrate_w_harmony`) |
| openpyxl | 3.1.5 | Excel parsing (sample loading table) |
| papermill | 2.6.0 | Parameterised notebook execution |

### Integration parameters (from `config.yaml`)

| Parameter | Value | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `min_genes` | 200 | Minimum genes per cell |
| `min_cells` | 5 | Minimum cells per gene |
| `n_top_genes` | 2000 | Highly variable genes selected |
| `batch_key` | `batch` | Key used for Harmony batch variable |
| `harmony_theta` | 2 | Harmony diversity clustering penalty |
| `harmony_dims` | 30 | PCA dimensions input to Harmony |
| `umap_dims` | 30 | Dimensions used for UMAP |
| `cluster_resolution` | 0.5 | Leiden clustering resolution |

---

## Stage 3 — Downstream Notebooks

Both notebooks are parameterised with Papermill and executed by the `parse_harmony_notebook` Snakemake rule. They run in the same `scvi-tools` conda environment as Stage 2 (all package versions identical to the table above).

### `inspect_integrated_anndata_bp.ipynb` (BP cohort)

Analyses the bisphenol (BP) treatment dataset.

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Input file | `output/scanpy/bp_harmony_integrated.h5ad` |
| Cells × HVGs | 28,915 × 2,000 |
| Batches | 21 (BPA/BPF/BPS × D7/D20 × dose levels + untreated controls) |
| Leiden clusters | 9 (resolution 0.5) |
| Batch correction key | `batch` |
| DE groupby variable | `condition` |
| Group 1 | `treat` |
| Group 2 | `control` |

**Analysis steps:**

1. Load pre-integrated AnnData (`.h5ad`)
2. PCA variance ratio inspection
3. UMAP without batch correction (30 PCs, 20 neighbours, `min_dist=0.3`)
4. Harmony-corrected UMAP (`X_pca_harmony` representation)
5. Leiden clustering on Harmony latent space (resolution 0.5)
6. Differential expression: `sc.tl.rank_genes_groups` (Wilcoxon) per cluster
7. Stacked bar plots of cluster × batch composition
8. Within-cluster DE between `treat` and `control` groups

---

### `inspect_integrated_anndata_nonbp.ipynb` (non-BP cohort)

Analyses the non-bisphenol treatment dataset.

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Input file | `output/scanpy/nonbp_harmony_integrated.h5ad` |
| Cells × HVGs | 44,156 × 2,000 |
| Batches | 9 (CH/OG/SM/WG × dose levels + untreated control) |
| Leiden clusters | 8 (resolution 0.5) |
| Batch correction key | `batch` |
| DE groupby variable | `condition` |
| Group 1 | `treat` |
| Group 2 | `control` |

**Analysis steps** (identical pipeline to the BP notebook):

1. Load pre-integrated AnnData
2. PCA variance ratio inspection
3. UMAP without batch correction
4. Harmony-corrected UMAP
5. Leiden clustering
6. `rank_genes_groups` (Wilcoxon) per cluster
7. Stacked bar plots of cluster × batch composition
8. Within-cluster DE between `treat` and `control`

---

## Conda Environments

The workflow uses two named conda environments:

| Env name | Primary use | Key packages |
|----------|------------|--------------|
| `spipe` | Parse split-pipe (FASTQ → count matrices) | split-pipe 1.6.0, Python 3.12.8 |
| `scvi-tools` | Integration, clustering, notebook execution | scanpy 1.11.4, harmonypy 0.0.10, scvi-tools 1.3.3, Python 3.12.11 |

The `scvi-tools` environment spec is defined in [`envs/scanpy_scvi.yaml`](../envs/scanpy_scvi.yaml); the full locked dependency list is in [`requirements.txt`](../requirements.txt).

---

## Reference Data

| Resource | Path | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| Mouse transcriptome (split-pipe) | `/dfs9/ucightf-lab/kstachel/TOOLS/REFERENCES/mouse/split-pipe/grcm38` | GRCm38 |
| Parse Singularity container | `/dfs7/singularity_containers/rcic/parsebio-1.0.3.sif` | parsebio 1.0.3 |
| Sample loading table | `metadata/*.xlsm` | Parse Biosciences Plate Configuration format |
| Sample list | `metadata/sample_list.txt` | Auto-generated from `.xlsm` by Snakemake |
